seoTitle: Insert a Checkbox in Confluence: 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to insert a checkbox in Confluence in 2026 using the Action Items block, assign owners and due dates, and add checkboxes inside table cells.
intro: Flat text task lists make it hard to track who owns what. This guide shows how to insert interactive checkboxes in Confluence in 2026, complete with assignees and due dates, so your team can track progress at a glance.

steps.heading: Deploy a checkbox
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/task` or `/action item` and hit Enter to drop a checkbox instantly.

steps.heading: Or use the toolbar
steps.body: Alternatively, click the visual checklist icon in the upper editor toolbar.

steps.heading: Use the keyboard shortcut
steps.body: Press Ctrl+Shift+T on Windows or Cmd+Shift+T on Mac to auto-generate a checkbox without a menu.

steps.heading: Assign an owner and date
steps.body: Type @ to assign a team member and // to select an active due date.

steps.heading: Add checkboxes inside tables
steps.body: Create a table and use the same slash command inside cells to track grid data with checkboxes.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Click Publish to launch the live interactive checklist for your workspace.

faq.question: Can I assign a checkbox item to a specific teammate?
faq.answer: Yes, typing @ inside an action item lets you assign it to a team member.

faq.question: Can I add a due date to a checkbox task?
faq.answer: Yes, typing // opens a date picker to set a due date for the item.

faq.question: Do assigned checkboxes show up anywhere else?
faq.answer: Yes, checkboxes link to user profile dashboards, aggregating pending actions automatically across the workspace.

faq.question: Can I put checkboxes inside a table instead of a plain list?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use the same task command inside table cells to track checkbox data within a structured grid.
